reasoning: Phase B Task B1 ships per-step error boundaries for every wizard segment (7 in total: onboarding, grand-idea, interview, architecture, proposal, design, atlas) plus a shared <WizardStepErrorBoundary> client component that owns the recovery UX + Tempo tracing emission. When a step segment throws, the framework mounts the segment's error.tsx (a 6-line shim) which mounts the shared boundary; the boundary emits one OTel span via @chiefaia/tracing v0.3.0 (createTracer + startSpan, manual ending — synchronous-commit-safe), surfaces the trace_id in the recovery copy so customer-support can quote it back, and renders three recovery CTAs (Try-again -> reset(), Back-to-dashboard -> window.location.assign('/'), Contact-support -> mailto with the trace_id pre-filled). Architecture segment is promoted from the dynamic [step] fallback to a first-class app/wizard/architecture/ directory — Next.js error.tsx only attaches to materialised segments. The placeholder page.tsx mirrors the [step] stub so UX is identical; B6 will replace its body without touching error.tsx. Reuse-first: every primitive comes from @caia/ui (Card+Button), every span goes through @chiefaia/tracing (no parallel @chiefaia/otel), no third-party error-boundary lib. Subscription-only: no HTTP client, no API keys, no remote-service reach. 18 new vitest cases cover rendering, recovery actions, and tracing. Pre-existing develop failures (B7/B3/B5 module-resolve + TS2352 in edge-bypass.test.ts) are unchanged — same tolerated set the backend B wave merged through.

reuseSearchResults.considered_for: Card + Button primitives for the recovery surface
reuseSearchResults.reason: Canonical UI primitive surface. Mandatory per ADR-065 and the caia-no-raw-shadcn-import-outside-ui-package semgrep rule — any raw shadcn/Radix import outside packages/ui would fail the reuse-advisory-blocking gate.

reuseSearchResults.considered_for: OTel span emission with wizard-step attributes
reuseSearchResults.reason: v0.3.0 already exports createTracer / startSpan / withSpan via the Wave-1 OTel bootstrap that B3 deepened. The boundary emits one span per mount with caia.wizard.step + error.name + error.message + error.digest attributes — same semantic-attribute shape B3 codified for the wizard API routes.
Canonical 7-step catalogue with type-only ProjectState import so compile-time drift between wizard and FSM throws. The boundary's STEP_TITLES record is keyed by the same slug union.

Logger is for backend Hono services + scripts. Client-side React component should not import a Pino-backed logger; the OTel span IS the trace record for Tempo + the digest is captured as a span attribute.
Next.js App Router supplies the error.tsx convention natively — no third-party error-boundary needed. Adding a dep would violate the 'check workspace first' rule and bloat the wizard bundle.

Boundary needs to render in jsdom test env without a NextRouter context; window.location.assign('/') works in both jsdom and prod. The onDashboardClick test seam lets unit tests assert the click handler without touching window.location.

modifications: Architecture segment promoted from the dynamic [step]/page.tsx fallback to a first-class app/wizard/architecture/ directory. Load-bearing because Next.js error.tsx only attaches to materialised segments — without the directory there is no architecture-specific boundary. The placeholder page.tsx mirrors the [step] stub so the UX is identical; B6 will replace its body without touching the sibling error.tsx.

Trace emission uses startSpan (not withSpan) inside a React commit because the boundary renders synchronously — withSpan would gate the commit on an async wrapper. Pattern matches the manual-span guidance in @chiefaia/tracing's README; the span is ended in the same commit pass.

traceImpl / onResetClick / onDashboardClick props exposed as test seams. The boundary degrades cleanly when no OTel SDK is installed (createTracer synthesises a stable traceId), so production paths never depend on the seams.

evidence.typecheckDelta: no new TS errors; pre-existing TS2352 + module-resolve set unchanged
